task_completion.description: Verifies the implementation of the CRC-16 calculation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Python file in the modbus/ directory open in an editor, containing bitwise operations or logic for CRC-16 calculation specifically using the polynomial 0xA001.

task_completion.description: Verifies the implementation of RTU frame construction and parsing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for Python code in the modbus/ directory showing byte manipulation, packing, or unpacking (e.g., using the 'struct' module) of Modbus RTU frames, which include address, function code, data, and CRC.

task_completion.description: Verifies the implementation of required Modbus Function Codes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for Python code in the modbus/ directory explicitly handling or routing Function Codes 01 (Read Coils), 03 (Read Holding Registers), 06 (Write Single Register), and 16 (Write Multiple Registers).

task_completion.description: Verifies the implementation of Modbus exception responses.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for Python code in the modbus/ directory defining, raising, or returning Modbus exception frames (typically involving adding 0x80 to the function code) and standard error codes.

task_completion.description: Verifies that the provided pytest suite was executed and passed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal window showing the execution of 'pytest' with output indicating that the tests passed (expecting up to 19 passing tests). The output should show a green passing status or a score meeting the threshold.

integrity.description: Verifies that no third-party Modbus libraries were used.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check the import statements in the visible Python files within the modbus/ directory. There should be no imports of external Modbus libraries like 'pymodbus', 'minimalmodbus', or 'umodbus'.

integrity.description: Verifies that the tests/ directory was not modified.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there is no visual evidence of the agent opening, editing, or saving files within the 'tests/' directory in the IDE/editor to force tests to pass.

integrity.description: Verifies that the test execution is genuine.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check the terminal output for standard pytest formatting, including the test session starts header, collected items count, execution time, and summary. It should not look like a manually typed text file or an echo command.
